title: Classic Borscht
description: A rich, hearty Eastern European beet soup with a deep ruby color. This traditional recipe combines tender vegetables, a savory broth, and a dollop of sour cream for a warming, satisfying meal.

steps.text: Peel and grate beets; sauté in oil with a splash of vinegar.
Dice onion and carrots; sauté in a separate pan until golden.
Add tomato paste to the onion-carrot mixture and cook 2 minutes.
Bring beef broth to a boil; add diced potatoes and cook.
Add shredded cabbage and cook another 10 minutes.
Add beets and the onion-carrot mixture; simmer.
Crush garlic and stir in; season with salt and pepper.
Serve hot topped with sour cream and fresh dill.

title: Spaghetti Carbonara
description: An authentic Roman pasta dish made with eggs, Pecorino Romano, guanciale, and black pepper. Creamy without any cream — just the magic of emulsified eggs and pasta water.

steps.text: Cook spaghetti in heavily salted boiling water until al dente; reserve 1 cup pasta water.
Dice guanciale and fry in a dry pan over medium heat until crispy; remove from heat.
Whisk eggs with finely grated Pecorino and Parmesan; season generously with black pepper.
Drain pasta and add directly to the guanciale pan (off heat).
Pour egg mixture over pasta, tossing quickly while adding pasta water splash by splash.
Toss vigorously until sauce is creamy and coats every strand.
Serve immediately with extra cheese and black pepper.

title: Greek Salad
description: A fresh, vibrant Mediterranean salad with crisp cucumbers, juicy tomatoes, creamy feta, and briny Kalamata olives dressed in olive oil and oregano. Ready in minutes.

steps.text: Cut tomatoes into wedges and cucumber into thick half-moons.
Slice red onion into thin rings; slice green pepper into strips.
Combine vegetables in a large bowl with olives.
Place feta on top in a large chunk or crumble.
Drizzle generously with olive oil; sprinkle with oregano, salt, and pepper.
Toss lightly just before serving.

title: Chicken Teriyaki
description: Tender chicken thighs glazed in a sweet-savory homemade teriyaki sauce. A Japanese-inspired weeknight favorite that comes together in under 30 minutes.

steps.text: Combine soy sauce, mirin, sake, sugar, garlic, and ginger to make the teriyaki sauce.
Marinate chicken in half the sauce for 15 minutes.
Heat a skillet over medium-high heat; cook chicken skin-side down until golden.
Flip chicken and cook another 5 minutes.
Pour remaining sauce over chicken; cook until glazed and sticky.
Slice and garnish with sesame seeds and green onions.
Serve with steamed rice.

title: Fluffy Pancakes
description: Light-as-air American-style pancakes with a golden crust and pillowy interior. Perfect for a lazy weekend breakfast with maple syrup and fresh berries.

steps.text: Whisk together flour, baking powder, sugar, and salt in a bowl.
In another bowl whisk eggs, milk, melted butter, and vanilla.
Pour wet ingredients into dry ingredients; stir until just combined — lumps are fine.
Rest batter 5 minutes while heating a non-stick pan over medium-low heat.
Lightly butter the pan; pour ¼ cup batter per pancake.
Cook until bubbles form and edges look set; flip and cook 1 minute more.
Serve with maple syrup and fresh berries.

title: Keto Avocado Bowl
description: A nutrient-dense, low-carb bowl built on creamy avocado with grilled salmon, leafy greens, cucumber, and a lemon-herb dressing. A complete meal that keeps you full for hours.

steps.text: Season salmon with salt, pepper, and lemon juice; grill or pan-fry.
Halve avocados; remove pit and scoop flesh into slices.
Arrange mixed greens in a bowl; add cucumber slices and halved cherry tomatoes.
Place salmon and avocado over the greens.
Drizzle with olive oil and remaining lemon juice.
Scatter fresh dill and season to taste.

title: Classic Tiramisu
description: The iconic Italian dessert with espresso-soaked ladyfingers and a cloud of mascarpone cream dusted with cocoa. No baking required — just patience while it sets overnight.

steps.text: Beat egg yolks with sugar until pale and thick.
Fold mascarpone into the yolk mixture until smooth.
Whip egg whites to stiff peaks; gently fold into mascarpone mixture.
Mix espresso with coffee liqueur in a shallow dish.
Quickly dip ladyfingers in espresso (1 second per side) and layer in a dish.
Spread half the mascarpone cream over the ladyfingers.
Repeat with another layer of soaked ladyfingers and remaining cream.
Refrigerate at least 4 hours (overnight is best).
Dust generously with cocoa powder before serving.

title: Smoothie Bowl
description: A thick, vibrant acai-mango smoothie bowl packed with antioxidants and topped with granola, fresh fruit, and seeds. A beautiful, nutritious breakfast that takes 10 minutes.

steps.text: Blend frozen acai, mango, banana, and almond milk until thick and smooth.
Pour into a bowl — the mixture should be thick enough to hold toppings.
Arrange granola, sliced strawberries, and kiwi on top.
Sprinkle chia seeds and drizzle with honey.

title: Beef Burger
description: A juicy smash-style beef burger with a crispy sear, melted cheddar, caramelized onions, and all the classic fixings. Better than anything from a fast-food chain.

steps.text: Slice onion; caramelize in butter over low heat.
Divide ground beef into 4 balls; season with salt and pepper.
Heat a cast-iron skillet until very hot; add beef balls and smash flat with a spatula.
Cook until edges are crispy; flip and immediately add cheese.
Cover for 1 minute to melt cheese; remove patties.
Toast buns in the same pan.
Assemble: sauce, lettuce, tomato, patty with cheese, caramelized onions, pickles.

title: Chicken Dumplings (Vareniki)
description: Homemade Ukrainian dumplings filled with seasoned ground chicken and onion, boiled until tender and served with butter and sour cream. A labor of love worth every minute.

steps.text: Mix flour, egg, water, and salt into a smooth dough; rest covered.
Combine ground chicken, grated onion, garlic, salt, and pepper for the filling.
Roll dough thinly; cut into circles with a glass.
Place 1 tsp filling in each circle; fold and pinch edges tightly.
Boil in salted water until dumplings float and are cooked through.
Drain and toss with melted butter.
Serve with sour cream.

title: Lentil Soup
description: A hearty, protein-rich red lentil soup spiced with cumin, turmeric, and smoked paprika. Vegan, filling, and ready in 30 minutes — perfect for a weeknight dinner.

steps.text: Dice onion, carrots, and garlic; sauté in olive oil until softened.
Add cumin, turmeric, and paprika; stir 1 minute until fragrant.
Add lentils, canned tomatoes, and broth; bring to a boil.
Reduce heat and simmer until lentils are completely soft.
Blend half the soup for a creamy-chunky texture (optional).
Stir in lemon juice; adjust seasoning.
Serve topped with fresh parsley.

title: Shakshuka
description: Eggs poached in a spiced tomato-pepper sauce with harissa and feta. A Middle-Eastern brunch staple that's bold, colorful, and on the table in 25 minutes.

steps.text: Dice onion and peppers; sauté in olive oil over medium heat.
Add garlic, cumin, paprika, and harissa; cook 1 minute.
Add crushed tomatoes; simmer until sauce thickens.
Make wells in the sauce with a spoon; crack one egg into each well.
Cover and cook until whites are set but yolks are still runny.
Crumble feta over the top; scatter fresh herbs.
Serve directly from the pan with crusty bread.
